title: The Charter
text: By what we are bound. The conduct expected of every member of the Assembly, from Pledge to Chief Librarian.

title: Welcome
text: A short introduction to the Librarians Assembly. What we are, what we do, and how to begin. A specialised collective within the Jedi Order. Keepers of the record, scholars of the Force.

title: The Charter
text: No member shall fabricate, distort, or knowingly omit truth from the Archive. The record stands above convenience. To falsify is to forfeit one's standing.

title: The Charter
text: Disagreement is welcome; contempt is not. All voices are heard before a verdict is taken. A member shall criticise an idea without diminishing the one who held it.

title: The Charter
text: Knowledge is held in trust, not in private. What you have learned, you are obligated to teach when asked. To hoard understanding is to misuse the standing the Assembly has granted you.

title: The Charter
text: Newcomers are owed mentorship, not impatience. Every member of the Assembly was once a Pledge. Treat them as you would have wished to be treated, and the Order is preserved.

title: The Charter
text: All standards of The Jedi Order apply within the Assembly. Harassment, prejudice, and conduct unbecoming a Jedi are not tolerated under any rank or pretext.

title: The Charter
text: A member's confidence is not for the marketplace. What is shared in good faith between keepers stays between keepers, save where the safety of another is at stake.

title: The Charter
text: Rank in the Assembly is earned, not granted. Authority over others is a duty held in service of the Order, never a privilege held over them.

title: The Charter
text: Where a member is called to account, the matter shall be heard openly by High Command. Decisions shall be recorded; reasoning shall be given. The Charter applies upward as well as downward.

title: The Charter
text: Any member may leave the Assembly with grace. Those who go are owed the Order's thanks for what they preserved. The doors close behind them; they do not close against them.

title: The Charter
text: "I have struck two members in my tenure for breach of this article. Neither returned." · Calex, 19th Chief Librarian

title: The Charter
text: "Read this slowly. Most who err here believe themselves the exception." · Thallor, 14th Chief Librarian

title: The Charter
text: "The greatest tutors I have known were those who feared most that they had nothing to teach." · Vael Orin, 11th Chief Librarian

title: The Charter
text: "I joined trembling, certain I would be turned away. The keeper who received me has since taught hundreds." · Anonymous, margin of the 7th edition

title: The Charter
text: "The robe does not excuse. It binds." · Calex, 19th Chief Librarian

title: The Charter
text: "The exception is narrow. Walk it carefully, and seek counsel." · Thallor, 14th Chief Librarian

title: The Charter
text: "Beware the Curator who finds the title heavier each year. Beware more the one who does not." · Vael Orin, 11th Chief Librarian

title: The Charter
text: "The hearing that absolved me also corrected me. I have been the better keeper since." · Anonymous, margin of the 12th edition

title: The Charter
text: "I have re-admitted three former keepers. None did so lightly. None did so unwelcomed." · Calex, 19th Chief Librarian

title: The Order
text: A specialised collective within the Jedi Order, devoted to the preservation, organisation, and dissemination of knowledge. Based in the Great Jedi Archives on Coruscant. Scholars, archivists, tutors. The four disciplines: Archive, Instruction, Discourse, Vigil.

title: The Order
text: Texts, recordings, and oral histories are gathered, indexed, and made available to those who would learn. The archive is the heart of the Order, without it, every other discipline collapses inward into mere opinion. The work of the Archive is not romantic. It is patient cataloguing, verification, cross-reference, and the slow labour of deciding what to keep and how. Every member of the Assembl...

title: The Order
text: From basic training to advanced symposia, members teach one another openly. No knowledge is hoarded; every Initiate may rise to Loremaster. What you have learned, you are obligated to teach when asked, this is not a courtesy of the Order, but a condition of belonging to it. Instruction in the Assembly takes many forms: lectures held in the great halls, one-on-one mentorship between keeper and ...

title: The Order
text: Through debate and assembly, contradictions in the record are weighed and resolved. Truth is found in argument, not in silence. The Assembly does not fear disagreement, it requires it. A record never challenged is a record never tested. Discourse within the Order is held to a high standard. A keeper may criticise an idea without diminishing the one who held it. All voices are heard before a ve...

title: The Order
text: The keepers of the Assembly patrol their holdings, defend their archives, and bear witness when history is being made. Knowledge unguarded is knowledge lost. What is preserved with patience can still be undone in a moment, and so the watch must be constant. The Vigil is the most outward-facing of the disciplines. Where the Archive looks inward to the catalogue and Instruction looks across to t...

title: The Path
text: How one rises. Ten ranks across four tiers, from Pledge to Chief Librarian. Rank in the Assembly is earned, not granted.

title: The Path
text: Pledges are unofficial members of the division who have recently passed the Librarians' Assembly application. They have full access to the Librarian robes and team. Pledges have one week to achieve Student and be accepted into the Assembly as a bonafide member. Requirements: Pass the application, OR be handpicked with reason by a member of High Command.

title: The Path
text: Students are full-fledged members of the Librarians' Assembly. The first of the low ranks, they may begin working towards advancing in the Assembly. Requirements: 1 submission (one chance of revision will be granted). Pass the orientation quiz. Must be a member of the Consular Pathway.

title: The Path
text: Librarians are low-ranks of the Assembly who have begun to prove themselves as members. They may now gear up for the rank of Intellect. Requirements: 3 submissions. Attend 4 trainings. Have been a Student for at least 1 day.

title: The Path
text: Intellects are the first official middle-rank of the Jedi Librarians' Assembly. They have gained enough knowledge and maturity to co-host training sessions. Requirements: 6 submissions. Attend 10 trainings. Attend a Patrol. Attend an Advanced Introductory Lecture. Attend 1 of: Seminar, Symposium, Debate, or Activity Push. Have been a Librarian for at least 3 days.

title: The Path
text: Tutors are highly respected and have gained much knowledge within the Assembly. They are permitted to assist the High Ranks. Requirements: 8 submissions. Attend 16 trainings. Co-host 5 events. Attend 3 of: Seminar, Symposium, Debate, Activity Push, or Patrol. Have been an Intellect for at least 5 days.

title: The Path
text: Lore Keepers are dedicated members devoted to the study and curation of the Jedi Archives. Strong scholars fulfilling the calling of wisdom and knowledge. Requirements: 10 submissions. Attend 20 trainings. Co-host 8 events. Attend 4 of: Seminar, Symposium, Debate, Activity Push, or Patrol. Receive 2 Divisional or higher Endorsements. Become Librarian of the Week at least once. Pass a High Comma...

title: The Path
text: Archivists are the Senior High Ranks of the Assembly. Held to the highest level of critical thinking and team management, they take charge in managing both the Assembly and the High Ranks, acting as mentors to all. Requirements: Have no active strikes within TJO or LA. Served as a Lore Keeper for two weeks (excluding the Probationary Period and any Inactivity Notices). Host 35 events. Review 20...

title: The Path
text: The Historian is a highly respected rank within the Jedi Order, acting as a role model to those of lower rank. They assist the Curator in overseeing the High Ranks, and report directly to the Curator of the Archives and Chief Librarian. Requirements: Be handpicked by the Chief Librarian and the Curator of the Archives.

title: The Path
text: Curator of the Archives is the second-in-command of the Librarians' Assembly. This person directly oversees the High Ranks and reports to the Chief Librarian. Requirements: Be handpicked by the Chief Librarian.

title: The Path
text: Chief Librarian is the highest achievable rank, in first command of the Librarians' Assembly. This person oversees the Assembly, the Jedi Archives, and serves on the Council of First Knowledge. Requirements: Be chosen by the Jedi High Council.

title: High Command
text: Those who steward the Order. The senior ranks of the Assembly. They guide instruction, preserve the record, and answer for the Order direction.

title: High Command
text:
Historians are the two junior members of the High Command team. They help
      oversee the daily functions, and act as mentors and firm hands for the
      High Rank team and the rest of the Institution. They are learning the
      reigns of leadership in the Assembly. They can usually help you with most
      issues you may have.

title: High Command
text:
The Curator of the Archives serves as the Second-in-Command of the
      Assembly, serving as a major supporter of the Chief Librarian; being
      qualified enough to step up in the event that the Chief Librarian is
      indisposed. Any concerns regarding a High Rank should be taken to them.

title: High Command
text:
The Chief Librarian is the Overseer and First-in-Command of the
      Librarians' Assembly. They serve on the Council of First Knowledge and
      relay the Assembly's concerns to the wider Council. Their main duty is to
      maintain the stability of the Assembly and lead it to a brighter future.

title: High Command
text: RuDz_Shark (@purplehaze9347), Historian.

title: High Command
text: Alexsnakk (@skullcruncher44), Historian.

title: High Command
text: PauVelaria (@pauvelaria), Curator of the Archives.

title: High Command
text: Valkorion9872 (@valkorion9872), Chief Librarian.

title: Petition for Membership
text: So you would join the Assembly. What is asked of those who would seek a place in the Order. Application requirements, the application form, what follows the petition, what you may ask of us.

title: Petition for Membership
section: · I · What is asked of you
text: Membership in the Assembly is granted by application, not by request, and not by chance. Before you petition, you must meet the following: Hold the rank of Initiate or above within the Order. Demonstrate a high level of grammatical ability in your written work. Carry yourself with the maturity the Assembly expects of its scholars. Be fluent in English, the working language of the Order. Be una...

title: Petition for Membership
text: The application is more than a single essay. It is a structured form that asks you to declare yourself, to reflect on what you bring to the Assembly, and to demonstrate your writing in your own words. Treat it as a piece of work, not a box to tick. The form covers, broadly: An integrity declaration. You will be asked to agree, plainly, that the work you submit is your own. Identifying details. ...

title: Petition for Membership
text: i. You join the Discord and read the rules of the Order. ii. You pend for the Roblox group linked from the Assembly Informations channel. iii. You complete and submit the application form, including its written sections. iv. A member of staff reviews your work. You are notified of the result with reasoning. v. If accepted, you are admitted as a Pledge and your path begins. A failed application ...

title: Petition for Membership
section: · IV · What you may ask of us
text: The Charter binds the Assembly to its members as much as its members to the Assembly. As a Pledge, and at every rank thereafter, you are owed: Mentorship. No one is left to find their footing alone. A clear path of advancement. Every rank, every requirement, written down and applied evenly. An honest hearing. If you are called to account, you will be heard openly and the reasoning given. A comm...

title: The Oath
text: By what we are sworn. The Oath of the Keeper, and what it asks of those who would take it. The origin of the Oath is not recorded. It has always been taken.

title: The Oath
text: The Oath is the moment a Pledge becomes a keeper. It is taken once at admission to the Assembly and held to thereafter. The words are short, the obligation long; this is by design. It is not a vow of perfect conduct, nor a promise to never err. It is simpler than that. The Oath is a declaration of what one intends to give to the Order, and what one accepts in return. To take it is to consent to...

title: The Oath
text: · Oath of the Keeper · I do pledge to keep the record by patience, by record, by light; to teach what I have learned, and to learn what I have not; to hold the Archive above convenience, and the Order above myself. · So sealed beneath the sigil ·

title: The Oath
text: Each line carries weight beyond its sentence. By patience, by record, by light is the motto of the Order, the first line names the disciplines a keeper accepts. To teach what I have learned, and to learn what I have not binds the keeper to instruction in both directions; one does not merely receive knowledge, nor merely give it. To hold the Archive above convenience is the most demanding claus...

title: The Oath
text: For those who would walk the Assembly's path, a keepsake. Inscribe your chosen name and receive the Oath in your own hand. Inscribe · Oath of the Keeper · I,, do pledge to keep the record by patience, by record, by light; to teach what I have learned, and to learn what I have not; to hold the Archive above convenience, and the Order above myself. · So sealed beneath the sigil · Copy as text Ta...

title: Voices
text: In their own words. What the keepers say of the Order, gathered by tier.

title: Voices
text: I joined expecting to be quiet for a long time before I had anything to add. I learned within a week that quiet is its own form of contribution, and that someone is always listening for it.

title: Voices
text: My first submission came back with three pages of notes. I was braced for criticism; what I got was attention. There is a difference, and I had not understood it before the Assembly.

title: Voices
text: Co-hosting changed how I read. Before, I read for myself. Now I read with the Pledge in mind who will ask me what a passage meant, and I find I understand it better when I have to be ready to explain it.

title: Voices
text: The Assembly is the rare place where being curious is treated as a contribution, not an interruption.

title: Voices
text: I came for the lore. I stayed because every conversation here teaches you something you didn't know you needed.

title: Voices
text: There's no other corner of the Order where the questions matter as much as the answers do.

title: Voices
text: The work doesn't get easier the higher you go. It gets quieter. The decisions land on you alone, more often, and the people you took the path with are now the people you must answer to honestly when they ask whether you are still the right hand for the work.

title: Voices
text: I have read every essay submitted in my tenure. Most teach me something. The ones that do not, teach me something about myself for what I had hoped to find.

title: Daily Reading
text: A passage from the holocron, drawn for the day. The same for all who read today. Different passage every day, deterministic by date.
